Details: Chromium (VI) removal by granular activated carbon can be very effective (up to 100 percent) under acidic pH range (2 to 6). However, average GAC service life ranged between 0.14 to 0.39 days. One study reported the optimum pH for chromium (VI) removal as 2 for the wood-based activated carbons and between 3 to 4 for coconut based carbons. In addition to solution pH, chromium (VI) removal is also influenced by contact time, initial chromium (VI) concentration, and carbon dosages.
